Title: Run a .NET Application as an Ubuntu Systemd Daemon
Description: Install and configure a .NET 8/9/10 application as a systemd service on Ubuntu

Sections.Content:
## Run a .NET Application as a Linux Daemon
This guide installs the .NET runtime and configures your .NET application as a systemd service so it starts automatically on boot.

### Step 1: Install the .NET Runtime on Ubuntu
Install the Microsoft package repository and then the runtime.

sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y wget apt-transport-https
wget https://packages.microsoft.com/config/ubuntu/$(lsb_release -rs)/packages-microsoft-prod.deb -O packages-microsoft-prod.deb
sudo dpkg -i packages-microsoft-prod.deb
rm packages-microsoft-prod.deb
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y dotnet-runtime-10.0
### Step 2: Create the application directory and publish your app

Copy your published .NET application files to /opt/$variable:AppName$/
You can use SCP or SFTP to transfer files.
### Step 3: Create the systemd service file

### Service file content:
Paste the following into the editor (adjust paths and user as needed):

```ini
[Unit]
Description=$variable:AppName$ .NET Service
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=notify
User=$username$
WorkingDirectory=/opt/$variable:AppName$
ExecStart=/usr/bin/dotnet /opt/$variable:AppName$/$variable:DllFileName$
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
SyslogIdentifier=$variable:AppName$
Environment=ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Production
Environment=DOTNET_PRINT_TELEMETRY_MESSAGE=false

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
### Step 4: Enable and start the service

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable $variable:AppName$
sudo systemctl start $variable:AppName$

sudo systemctl stop $variable:AppName$
sudo systemctl restart $variable:AppName$
sudo systemctl disable $variable:AppName$
